Just now

If You’re Still Prompting, You Don’t Actually Understand AI.
SourceIn 1780, running a steam engine didn’t just require coal. It required a teenager.
They were called “valve boys.”
These boys pulled heavy iron levers on a high-stakes three-second loop to keep the boilers from stalling or exploding
Naturally, these boys believed they were irreplaceable. They mistook constant anxiety for irreplaceable value.
Then, in 1788, James Watt invented the Centrifugal Governor.
It was a simple, elegant device with two spinning metal balls.
When the engine ran too fast, centrifugal force flung the balls outward, automatically closing the steam valve. When it slowed, gravity pulled them back down, opening it.
Overnight, the job of valve boys was annihilated.
Watt made their entire labor philosophy look like a joke.
Look in the mirror. Look at your glowing screen and your tired eyes.
You type 500-word paragraphs begging for a frontend component, then sit there manually correcting the mistakes over and over.
You think you have mastered these AI tools.
